title: Data storage device with closed loop extended park mode during spin down operation
abstract: A data storage device has a closed loop extended park mode during spin down operation. A data storage device comprises a spindle motor configured to rotate one or more disks, and one or more processing devices. The one or more processing devices are configured to determine a value of current that is discharged from the spindle motor over time during a spin down of the spindle motor, and control a braking duty cycle for braking the spindle motor during the spin down such that the value of current discharged from the spindle motor over time does not exceed a selected current limit.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A data storage device comprising: a spindle motor configured to rotate one or more disks; and one or more processing devices configured to: determine a value of current that is discharged from the spindle motor over time during a spin down of the spindle motor; and control a braking duty cycle for braking the spindle motor during the spin down such that the value of current discharged from the spindle motor over time does not exceed a selected current limit. 2. The data storage device of claim 1, wherein the one or more processing devices are further configured to control the braking duty cycle such that the value of current discharged through the spindle motor over time remains at or below a maximum current rating of the spindle motor. 3. The data storage device of claim 1, wherein the one or more processing devices are further configured to set the selected current limit at a value below a maximum current rating of the spindle motor. 4. The data storage device of claim 3, wherein the maximum current rating of the spindle motor is approximately 3 amps, and the one or more processing devices are further configured to set the selected current limit at a value that is less than 3 amps. 5. The data storage device of claim 1, wherein the current discharged from the spindle motor is discharged through one or more low-side switches of the spindle motor, and wherein determining the value of current discharged from the spindle motor comprises determining a value of current through the one or more low-side switches of the spindle motor. 6. The data storage device of claim 5, wherein the one or more processing devices are further configured to use a feedback loop to set the braking duty cycle, the feedback loop comprising a proportional integral controller configured to modify the braking duty cycle based on feedback of the determined value of current through the one or more low-side switches of the 

spindle motor, such that the current through the one or more low-side switches remains at or below the selected current limit of the spindle motor. 7. The data storage device of claim 1, further comprising a voice coil motor (VCM) that comprises left and right low-side switches, wherein the one or more processing devices are further configured to shunt a portion of the current discharged from the spindle motor during a spin down of the spindle motor through both the left and right low-side switches comprised in the VCM. 8. The data storage device of claim 7, wherein the one or more processing devices are further configured to load-balance the portion of the current shunted through the left and right low-side switches. 9. A method comprising: determining, by one or more processing devices, a value of current that is discharged from a spindle motor of a data storage device over time during a spin down of the spindle motor; and controlling, by the one or more processing devices, a braking duty cycle for braking the spindle motor during the spin down such that the value of current discharged from the spindle motor over time does not exceed a selected current limit. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising controlling the braking duty cycle such that the value of current discharged through the spindle motor over time remains at or below a maximum current rating of the spindle motor. 11. The method of claim 9, further comprising setting the selected current limit at a value below a maximum current rating of the spindle motor. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the maximum current rating of the spindle motor is approximately 3 amps, the method further comprising setting the selected current limit at a value that is less than 3 amps. 13. The method of claim 9, wherein the current discharged from the spindle motor is discharged through one or more low-side switches of the spindle motor, and wherein 

determining the value of current discharged from the spindle motor comprises determining a value of current through the one or more low-side switches of the spindle motor. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising using a feedback loop to set the braking duty cycle, the feedback loop comprising a proportional integral controller configured to modify the braking duty cycle based on feedback of the determined value of current through the one or more low-side switches of the spindle motor, such that the current through the one or more low-side switches remains at or below the selected current limit of the spindle motor. 15. The method of claim 9, further comprising shunting a portion of the current discharged from the spindle motor during a spin down of the spindle motor through both left and right low- side switches comprised in a voice coil motor (VCM) of the data storage device. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising load-balancing the portion of the current shunted through the left and right low-side switches. 17. One or more processing devices comprising: means for determining a value of current that is discharged from a spindle motor of a data storage device over time during a spin down of the spindle motor; and means for controlling a braking duty cycle for braking the spindle motor during the spin down such that the value of current discharged from the spindle motor over time does not exceed a selected current limit. 18. The one or more processing devices of claim 17, further comprising means for controlling the braking duty cycle such that the value of current discharged through the spindle motor over time remains at or below a maximum current rating of the spindle motor. 19. The one or more processing devices of claim 17, wherein the current discharged from the spindle motor is discharged through one or more low-side switches of the spindle motor, and wherein determining the value of current discharged from the spindle motor comprises determining a value of current through the one or more low-side switches of the spindle motor. 20. The one or more processing devices of claim 17, further comprising means for shunting a portion of the current discharged from the spindle motor during a spin down of the spindle motor 16 

through both left and right low-side switches comprised in a voice coil motor (VCM) of the data storage device. 17
